name: Community First Bank, National Association
description_short: Community First Bank, N.A. is an FDIC-insured community bank operating in Ohio with multiple branch locations offering personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Community First Bank, National Association is a federally chartered community bank headquartered in Forest, Ohio, with branch locations in Forest, Upper Sandusky, and Kenton. As an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, the bank has positioned itself as a local financial services provider serving individuals and businesses across northwest Ohio.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of deposit products including personal checking and savings accounts, health savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, and money market accounts. On the business side, Community First Bank provides business checking, business savings, business money market accounts, and business CDs. The bank also extends lending services including personal loans, business loans, and agriculture loans—reflecting its regional focus on supporting both consumer and agricultural customers. Digital banking capabilities include online banking, mobile banking with check deposit functionality, and business cash management services.

Community First Bank distinguishes itself through its local presence and specialized lending in agriculture, which aligns with the rural and semi-rural character of its Ohio service area. The bank has invested in modern digital infrastructure, recently redesigning its website to improve mobile and desktop usability. The presence of a dedicated lending team and emphasis on personalized service differentiate it from larger national banks, though the company's regional footprint remains limited.

As a small community bank, Community First Bank serves customers seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making and agriculture-focused lending. However, the bank's limited branch network and regional focus mean customers outside northwest Ohio cannot access in-person services. The website provides basic service information but lacks specific product details, rates, or online application portals that larger competitors offer. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking app with mobile check deposit
Business online banking and cash management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit backing from U.S. Government
Specialized agriculture lending through dedicated lending team for farm-related financing needs
Free mobile banking app with mobile check deposit functionality for convenience
Multiple convenient branch locations across Forest, Upper Sandusky, and Kenton, Ohio
Comprehensive personal and business banking products under one institution
Recently redesigned website optimized for all devices and platforms
Member FDIC with Equal Housing Lender certification demonstrating regulatory compliance
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to three Ohio towns, making branch access unavailable for most U.S. consumers
Website lacks specific interest rates, APYs, fees, or detailed product terms for comparison shopping
No indication of online account opening or application capabilities from the website content
Small community bank may have fewer digital features, lower CD rates, or higher minimum balances than national competitors
Limited visibility into lending approval criteria, loan terms, or agriculture lending requirements
best_for: Rural and agricultural customers in northwest Ohio seeking farm financing and locally-based lending decisions
Small business owners in the Forest, Upper Sandusky, and Kenton areas needing personalized business banking relationships
Community residents prioritizing local banking with face-to-face service and branch accessibility
Farmers and agricultural operations requiring specialized lending products and rural banking expertise
diagnosis: Community First Bank is best for individuals, families, and agricultural businesses in northwest Ohio seeking relationship-based community banking with local branch access and specialized farm lending. The primary caveat is severe geographic limitation—this bank only serves three specific Ohio towns, making it inaccessible for consumers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
